messages.content:
Assessment in inclusive classrooms is one of the learning barriers experienced by learners with impairments due to inaccessible testing materials, which discriminate against those with needs that require accommodation
Select one:
a. Maybe
b. True
c. False
d. None of the options
b. True

___________________ creates room for equal opportunities for all learners within the school system
Select one:
a. The government
b. Inclusive curriculum
c. Teachers
d. Curriculum
b. Inclusive curriculum

_____________ places special emphasis on the previously marginalised population, including people with disabilities, children, children of migrant workers and indigenous populations,
Select one:
a. UNESCO
b. WHO
c. UN
d. SADC
a. UNESCO

___________________ should be given an opportunity to access learning and teaching activities in a variety of ways
Select one:
a. Parents
b. Learners
c. Lecturers
d. Teachers
b. Learners

Education White Paper 6 recognised that every learner had the ability to learn and that support should be provided to meet each learner's unique/diverse needs
Select one:
a. True
b. Maybe
c. False
d. None of the options
a. True

A learner with visual impairment may require
Select one:
a. Sign language
b. Written text or magnifying lenses
c. Lip-reading skills
d. Large-print texts or magnifying lenses
d. Large-print texts or magnifying lenses

Getting peers to interact with one another, and to work alongside each other despite different abilities and needs, is known by which of the following terms
Select one:
a. Leading by example
b. Forced interaction
c. Peer pressure
d. Cooperative learning
d. Cooperative learning

The medical model believes that the problem is within the
Select one:
a. Classroom
b. Schooling system
c. Learner
d. Teacher
c. Learner

What are some of the causes of xenophobia in South Africa
Select one:
a. Distrust and fear of foreigners
b. Poor access to economic opportunities
c. A lack of social integration between locals and foreigners
d. Threat of conflict between South Africa and the rest of Africa

a. Distrust and fear of foreigners
b. Poor access to economic opportunities
c. A lack of social integration between locals and foreigners
d. Threat of conflict between South Africa and the rest of Africa

What are some of the causes of xenophobia in South Africa
Select one:
a. Distrust and fear of foreigners
b. Poor access to economic opportunities
c. A lack of social integration between locals and foreigners
d. Threat of conflict between South Africa and the rest of Africa

a. Distrust and fear of foreigners
b. Poor access to economic opportunities
c. A lack of social integration between locals and foreigners
d. Threat of conflict between South Africa and the rest of Africa

What are some of the causes of xenophobia in South Africa
Select one:
a. Distrust and fear of foreigners
b. Poor access to economic opportunities
c. A lack of social integration between locals and foreigners
d. Threat of conflict between South Africa and the rest of Africa

a. Distrust and fear of foreigners
b. Poor access to economic opportunities
c. A lack of social integration between locals and foreigners
d. Threat of conflict between South Africa and the rest of Africa
